page: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance configured with 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. The underlying hardware features an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, providing 16 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering a substantial capacity for data-intensive operations without requiring external block storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong memory subsystem, with Passmark memory scores placing in the top 25% (strong tier). Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is highly competitive, ranking in the top 10% (top tier). However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks, such as large-block read and write operations, fall into the average tier. This indicates that while individual core execution is highly efficient, the shared CPU allocation may introduce resource contention under sustained multi-core workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high density of memory and local NVMe storage relative to its virtual CPU count, making it a viable option for workloads requiring large datasets in memory without the premium of dedicated CPU instances. This resource profile is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, caching layers like Redis, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from fast local storage but do not require sustained, non-shared CPU performance.
description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with a shared CPU allocation, this server includes 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory performance, though multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it a cost-effective option for memory-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications that benefit from fast local storage but do not demand dedicated, non-shared CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage on a shared CPU allocation.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is a shared-CPU instance featuring 16 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-capacity memory and NVMe storage instance featuring AMD EPYC processors for memory-intensive and database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB RAM providing a high 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio
1600 GB of fast local NVMe SSD storage bundled directly with the instance
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory benchmark results
AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz with shared CPU allocation
Best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications
